52

00:02:08,310  -->  00:02:10,830
which means that you could go ahead and start testing them
53

53

00:02:10,830  -->  00:02:12,840
for bugs and vulnerabilities.
54

54

00:02:12,840  -->  00:02:14,700
But like I said, you should leave that for later,
55

55

00:02:14,700  -->  00:02:16,920
until you actually have a better understanding
56

56

00:02:16,920  -->  00:02:19,680
of the whole process and how the communication happens
57

57

00:02:19,680  -->  00:02:21,240
once you discover a bug.
58

58

00:02:21,240  -->  00:02:23,010
So I highly recommend starting
59

59

00:02:23,010  -->  00:02:25,710
with a bug bounty platform that, like I said,
60

60

00:02:25,710  -->  00:02:29,160
makes the whole process much easier and more fun.
61

61

00:02:29,160  -->  00:02:31,500
Again, there's a lot of bug bounty platforms.
62

62

00:02:31,500  -->  00:02:35,130
So all you have to do is look for bug bounty platform
63

63

00:02:35,130  -->  00:02:37,440
or platforms if you want to get a list of them.
64

64

00:02:37,440  -->  00:02:38,850
There is so many of them.
65

65

00:02:38,850  -->  00:02:42,120
I will include the list in the resources as well myself.
66

66

00:02:42,120  -->  00:02:46,200
But the main one and the most popular one is HackerOne,
67

67

00:02:46,200  -->  00:02:48,390
which you probably already heard of.
68

68

00:02:48,390  -->  00:02:50,370
And once you go to their website,
69

69

00:02:50,370  -->  00:02:53,490
you will notice that it might be a little bit confusing.
70

70

00:02:53,490  -->  00:02:54,630
And the reason for that,
71

71

00:02:54,630  -->  00:02:57,390
because they are trying to target the companies.
72

72

00:02:57,390  -->  00:03:00,840
That's why the website is styled and designed in a way
73

73

00:03:00,840  -->  00:03:04,080
that it seems like it's really hard to sign up for and use,
74

74

00:03:04,080  -->  00:03:07,200
because it's designed to get companies to sign up,
75

75

00:03:07,200  -->  00:03:08,190
not hackers.
76

76

00:03:08,190  -->  00:03:11,760
Now, a quick note before I continue covering this platform
77

77

00:03:11,760  -->  00:03:13,380
Don't get intimidated by it.
78

78

00:03:13,380  -->  00:03:16,530
This is the biggest bug bounty platform right now.
79

79

00:03:16,530  -->  00:03:19,380
You will probably not gonna start using it straight away.
80

80

00:03:19,380  -->  00:03:22,170
There are other simpler bug bounty platforms
81

81

00:03:22,170  -->  00:03:23,490
that you should start with,
82

82

00:03:23,490  -->  00:03:25,860
but I'm just covering this to show you how to use it.
83

83

00:03:25,860  -->  00:03:29,550
Because like I said, it is the biggest bug bounty platform.
84

84

00:03:29,550  -->  00:03:31,230
So the first thing you want to do is sign up
85

85

00:03:31,230  -->  00:03:32,063
with this website.
86

86

00:03:32,063  -->  00:03:33,810
Just like any other website that you use,
87

87

00:03:33,810  -->  00:03:35,760
you need to create an account on it.
88

88

00:03:35,760  -->  00:03:38,910
So you can simply just click on Login.
89

89

00:03:38,910  -->  00:03:40,230
And here at the bottom,
90

90

00:03:40,230  -->  00:03:42,300
you have a link to create an account.
91

91

00:03:42,300  -->  00:03:44,670
So you're just gonna click on Create account.
92

92

00:03:44,670  -->  00:03:47,580
And it's asking you, are you a hacker or a company?
93

93

00:03:47,580  -->  00:03:49,020
So we are hackers.
94

94

00:03:49,020  -->  00:03:50,400
We want to start discovering bugs.
95

95

00:03:50,400  -->  00:03:52,680
So we're gonna click on a hacker account.
96

96

00:03:52,680  -->  00:03:54,900
The rest of the process is very, very simple.
97

97

00:03:54,900  -->  00:03:58,440
You have to fill up your name, a username, your email,
98

98

00:03:58,440  -->  00:04:00,540
put a password, create an account,
99

99

00:04:00,540  -->  00:04:02,880
and then it's gonna send you a verification email.
100

100

00:04:02,880  -->  00:04:04,170
You have to verify it,
101

101

00:04:04,170  -->  00:04:06,570
and then you'll have your account properly registered
102

102

00:04:06,570  -->  00:04:09,090
and verified on this website.
103

103

00:04:09,090  -->  00:04:10,560
I'm not gonna cover that process
104

104

00:04:10,560  -->  00:04:12,840
because it is very, very simple.
105

105

00:04:12,840  -->  00:04:16,350
Once you have an account, you can simply click on Login
106

106

00:04:16,350  -->  00:04:19,200
and put in your username and your password.
107

107

00:04:19,200  -->  00:04:21,450
You're gonna click on Sign in.
108

108

00:04:21,450  -->  00:04:22,530
And once you sign in,
109

109

00:04:22,530  -->  00:04:24,960
because we signed in to a hacker's account,
110

110

00:04:24,960  -->  00:04:27,480
you will see that the platform kind of changes
111

111

00:04:27,480  -->  00:04:30,270
and becomes more geared towards a hacker,
112

112

00:04:30,270  -->  00:04:31,830
towards someone that actually wants
113

113

00:04:31,830  -->  00:04:34,350
to discover bugs and vulnerabilities.
114

114

00:04:34,350  -->  00:04:36,720
So as you can see, you automatically get redirected
115

115

00:04:36,720  -->  00:04:38,730
to the Opportunities page,
116

116

00:04:38,730  -->  00:04:41,760
where you can see all of the bug bounty programs available
117

117

00:04:41,760  -->  00:04:43,320
on this website.
118

118

00:04:43,320  -->  00:04:44,910
And we have the search bar in here,
119

119

00:04:44,910  -->  00:04:48,090
which you could use to search for a specific program.
120

120

00:04:48,090  -->  00:04:51,870
You could also filter based on the type of the program.
121

121

00:04:51,870  -->  00:04:55,350
So BBP is short for bug bounty program.
122

122

00:04:55,350  -->  00:04:58,350
VDP is a vulnerability disclosure program.
123

123

00:04:58,350  -->  00:05:01,020
And then they also offer private programs.
124

124

00:05:01,020  -->  00:05:03,300
You could search based on the asset type.
125

125

00:05:03,300  -->  00:05:06,030
So, for example, if you want to specify the domain,
126

126

00:05:06,030  -->  00:05:09,570
if you're looking for iOS apps or Android apps
127

127

00:05:09,570  -->  00:05:12,510
or websites and other, all kinds of stuff.
128

128

00:05:12,510  -->  00:05:14,370
So like I said, this is a huge platform
129

129

00:05:14,370  -->  00:05:17,070
that has all kinds of bug bounty programs.
130

130

00:05:17,070  -->  00:05:18,630
You can even select the industry,
131

131

00:05:18,630  -->  00:05:20,970
like automotive or computer software.
132

132

00:05:20,970  -->  00:05:23,730
And as you can see, you have a big list in here.
133

133

00:05:23,730  -->  00:05:25,290
Or if you don't want to do any of this,
134

134

00:05:25,290  -->  00:05:26,940
you can simply just scroll down
135

135

00:05:26,940  -->  00:05:30,150
and see programs based on the industry,
136

136

00:05:30,150  -->  00:05:32,670
based on the bounties.
137

137

00:05:32,670  -->  00:05:35,460
You can see assets that you might be interested in.
138

138

00:05:35,460  -->  00:05:38,910
So this is basically just a discovery page
139

139

00:05:38,910  -->  00:05:42,210
where you could find bug bounty programs to participate in.
140

140

00:05:42,210  -->  00:05:44,580
And if you don't find this very useful,
141

141

00:05:44,580  -->  00:05:47,493
you could also go to the directory.
142

142

00:05:48,780  -->  00:05:50,640
And it'll actually just list the programs
143

143

00:05:50,640  -->  00:05:52,200
in a nice list view.
144

144

00:05:52,200  -->  00:05:54,720
So this might be a bit simpler for you to go through.
145

145

00:05:54,720  -->  00:05:55,620
And as you can see,
146

146

00:05:55,620  -->  00:05:58,290
you can already notice a lot of famous websites,
147

147

00:05:58,290  -->  00:06:00,753
like Amazon, GameStop, LinkedIn,
148

148

00:06:02,070  -->  00:06:03,060
and the list goes on.
149

149

00:06:03,060  -->  00:06:04,470
They have PayPal. They have Tesla.
150

150

00:06:04,470  -->  00:06:06,870
They have everybody in here, eToro.
151

151

00:06:06,870  -->  00:06:08,370
So you should go through this list
152

152

00:06:08,370  -->  00:06:11,610
and see which program is most appropriate for you.
153

153

00:06:11,610  -->  00:06:14,280
And you can decide that by clicking on the program
154

154

00:06:14,280  -->  00:06:16,140
to get more information about it.
155

155

00:06:16,140  -->  00:06:19,110
And you can see, for example, the rewards that they give.
156

156

00:06:19,110  -->  00:06:20,880
So for a low vulnerability,
157

157

00:06:20,880  -->  00:06:23,280
you get about 100 to 250.
158

158

00:06:23,280  -->  00:06:26,430
For a medium, you get 250 to 2,500.
159

159

00:06:26,430  -->  00:06:29,520
For high and for critical, you get these values.
160

160

00:06:29,520  -->  00:06:31,320
So from here, you can get an idea,
161

161

00:06:31,320  -->  00:06:32,850
do I want to invest time
162

162

00:06:32,850  -->  00:06:34,920
into discovering bugs in this platform?
163

163

00:06:34,920  -->  00:06:36,090
Because, for example,
164

164

00:06:36,090  -->  00:06:38,160
some platforms might not even give money.
165

165

00:06:38,160  -->  00:06:40,140
They might just give you points or swag,
166

166

00:06:40,140  -->  00:06:42,960
like t-shirts or stickers and so on.
167

167

00:06:42,960  -->  00:06:45,090
So you might not want to invest your time in that.
168

168

00:06:45,090  -->  00:06:46,860
You might only want to get money.
169

169

00:06:46,860  -->  00:06:51,090
So it really depends on your criteria, on your requirement.
170

170

00:06:51,090  -->  00:06:53,010
But as someone that is only starting,
171

171

00:06:53,010  -->  00:06:55,290
you should actually be looking for programs
172

172

00:06:55,290  -->  00:06:57,060
that do not pay a lot of money.
173

173

00:06:57,060  -->  00:06:58,110
It might seem stupid
174

174

00:06:58,110  -->  00:07:00,750
because obviously you want to make the most money possible.
175

175

00:07:00,750  -->  00:07:03,810
But the thing is, the programs with the higher rewards
176

176

00:07:03,810  -->  00:07:05,850
will attract the best hackers out there.
177

177

00:07:05,850  -->  00:07:08,100
And there are some amazing hackers out there
178

178

00:07:08,100  -->  00:07:09,570
that do this full time.
179

179

00:07:09,570  -->  00:07:12,180
So even if you actually manage to discover a bug
180

180

00:07:12,180  -->  00:07:15,390
or a vulnerability in these platforms and send it,
181

181

00:07:15,390  -->  00:07:18,150
these really good hackers have probably discovered it
182

182

00:07:18,150  -->  00:07:19,530
and sent it before you.
183

183

00:07:19,530  -->  00:07:23,190
And therefore, your bug will be considered as a duplicate.
184

184

00:07:23,190  -->  00:07:25,320
And as a result, you will not be paid.
185

185

00:07:25,320  -->  00:07:28,470
Also, programs with high rewards are usually run
186

186

00:07:28,470  -->  00:07:30,750
by more secure websites.
187

187

00:07:30,750  -->  00:07:32,970
And by that, I don't mean that they're not vulnerable.
188

188

00:07:32,970  -->  00:07:34,740
I just mean that they are more challenging,
189

189

00:07:34,740  -->  00:07:36,210
and it's harder to discover bugs
190

190

00:07:36,210  -->  00:07:37,800
and vulnerabilities in them.
191

191

00:07:37,800  -->  00:07:40,260
Therefore, it's best to start with programs
192

192

00:07:40,260  -->  00:07:41,910
that are not very popular,
193

193

00:07:41,910  -->  00:07:43,470
that do not give a lot of rewards,
194

194

00:07:43,470  -->  00:07:45,840
so that your chances of actually discovering bugs
195

195

00:07:45,840  -->  00:07:48,030
and getting a bounty for it is high.
196

196

00:07:48,030  -->  00:07:51,120
And then build up your experience, build up your confidence,
197

197

00:07:51,120  -->  00:07:53,523
and then move your way up the ladder from there.
198

198

00:07:54,450  -->  00:07:56,880
Scrolling down, you're gonna get the policy
199

199

00:07:56,880  -->  00:07:59,670
for this particular bug bounty program.
200

200

00:07:59,670  -->  00:08:01,470
Now, this is different depending
201

201

00:08:01,470  -->  00:08:03,330
on the program that you selected.
202

202

00:08:03,330  -->  00:08:04,910
So the policy that you see in here
203

203

00:08:04,910  -->  00:08:07,590
in LinkedIn is gonna be completely different
204

204

00:08:07,590  -->  00:08:09,420
than the policy that you're gonna see
205

205

00:08:09,420  -->  00:08:10,500
in a different website,
206

206

00:08:10,500  -->  00:08:12,690
for example, Linktree in here.
207

207

00:08:12,690  -->  00:08:15,000
As you can see, even the rewards are different.
208

208

00:08:15,000  -->  00:08:17,580
So that's why you want to go through all of these programs
209

209

00:08:17,580  -->  00:08:20,310
and see the rewards, the policy.
210

210

00:08:20,310  -->  00:08:23,310
And before you start engaging, it is very important
211

211

00:08:23,310  -->  00:08:26,340
to understand this policy very, very well.
212

212

00:08:26,340  -->  00:08:28,230
So you want to make sure you understand the rules
213

213

00:08:28,230  -->  00:08:29,640
and you don't break them.
214

214

00:08:29,640  -->  00:08:31,440
You want to make sure that you test the things
215

215

00:08:31,440  -->  00:08:33,060
that are in scope.
216

216

00:08:33,060  -->  00:08:36,960
So if we scroll in here, it tells you what you can test.
217

217

00:08:36,960  -->  00:08:39,180
So only the assets mentioned in here,
218

218

00:08:39,180  -->  00:08:42,330
only the domains mentioned in here are eligible.
219

219

00:08:42,330  -->  00:08:44,190
So as you can see, you have all of the domains
220

220

00:08:44,190  -->  00:08:47,190
that are eligible for testing.
221

221

00:08:47,190  -->  00:08:49,620
If you scroll down, you also see the stuff
222

222

00:08:49,620  -->  00:08:51,150
that is out of the scope.
223

223

00:08:51,150  -->  00:08:54,570
So these assets and these domains should not be tested.
224

224

00:08:54,570  -->  00:08:57,420
And even if you discover a very critical bug
225

225

00:08:57,420  -->  00:08:59,700
or vulnerability in these assets,
226

226

00:08:59,700  -->  00:09:01,590
you will not get a bounty.
227

227

00:09:01,590  -->  00:09:03,870
And you will also be breaking the law
228

228

00:09:03,870  -->  00:09:05,490
because they're clearly telling you
229

229

00:09:05,490  -->  00:09:07,380
that this is out of scope.
230

230

00:09:07,380  -->  00:09:09,870
And there is nothing more annoying to a company
231

231

00:09:09,870  -->  00:09:13,980
or a researcher that is running a bug bounty platform
232

232

00:09:13,980  -->  00:09:15,840
other than getting a report
233

233

00:09:15,840  -->  00:09:19,080
on something that they clearly told you it's out of scope.
234

234

00:09:19,080  -->  00:09:21,450
So we don't care if it's vulnerable.
235

235

00:09:21,450  -->  00:09:23,160
We don't care if it's very scary.
236

236

00:09:23,160  -->  00:09:26,250
We tell you it's out of scope, it means do not test it.
237

237

00:09:26,250  -->  00:09:28,110
So it's very important to only test the stuff
238

238

00:09:28,110  -->  00:09:31,470
that is in scope, and it's also very important
239

239

00:09:31,470  -->  00:09:33,570
to test the vulnerabilities that they tell you.
240

240

00:09:33,570  -->  00:09:36,007
So sometimes in here, they would actually tell you,
241

241

00:09:36,007  -->  00:09:38,190
"I do not want excessive vulnerabilities."
242

242

00:09:38,190  -->  00:09:40,530
Now, that's highly unlikely, but it's possible.
243

243

00:09:40,530  -->  00:09:41,363
Or they might tell you,
244

244

00:09:41,363  -->  00:09:43,620
"I do not want remote code execution."
245

245

00:09:43,620  -->  00:09:44,970
So even if you manage to get it
246

246

00:09:44,970  -->  00:09:46,770
and you spend months trying to get it,
247

247

00:09:46,770  -->  00:09:49,950
you will not get your bounty because we clearly told you
248

248

00:09:49,950  -->  00:09:52,620
that we do not want these vulnerabilities.
249

249

00:09:52,620  -->  00:09:54,390
So that's a quick look on HackerOne.
250

250

00:09:54,390  -->  00:09:56,190
I highly recommend creating an account
251

251

00:09:56,190  -->  00:09:57,540
and going through the programs.
252

252

00:09:57,540  -->  00:10:00,750
But like I said, do not get intimidated by this platform.
253

253

00:10:00,750  -->  00:10:02,010
This is the biggest one,
254

254

00:10:02,010  -->  00:10:04,140
and it has really big bug bounty programs,
255

255

00:10:04,140  -->  00:10:06,600
ones that pay you hundreds of thousands of dollars.
256

256

00:10:06,600  -->  00:10:08,670
So you probably will not be using this.
257

257

00:10:08,670  -->  00:10:10,920
Like I said, you will be using this maybe
258

258

00:10:10,920  -->  00:10:12,450
later on in the future.
259

259

00:10:12,450  -->  00:10:15,180
But I just wanted to give you a quick walkthrough
260

260

00:10:15,180  -->  00:10:16,260
over it right now.
261

261

00:10:16,260  -->  00:10:19,140
And I am gonna show you simpler bug bounty programs
262

262

00:10:19,140  -->  00:10:20,160
in the next lecture,
263

263

00:10:20,160  -->  00:10:22,350
where you should be starting your journey
264

264

00:10:22,350  -->  00:10:25,053
into becoming an expert bug hunter.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1
1

00:00:00,000  -->  00:00:01,950
<v Instructor>As mentioned in the previous lecture,</v>
2

2

00:00:01,950  -->  00:00:04,380
HackerOne is a really nice platform,
3

3

00:00:04,380  -->  00:00:05,850
but it's really big
4

4

00:00:05,850  -->  00:00:08,460
and that's why it might be intimidating
5

5

00:00:08,460  -->  00:00:11,190
for someone that is just starting bug hunting,
6

6

00:00:11,190  -->  00:00:12,510
like yourself.
7

7

00:00:12,510  -->  00:00:14,460
That's why you should create an account
8

8

00:00:14,460  -->  00:00:15,510
and have a look at it,
9

9

00:00:15,510  -->  00:00:17,970
but do not limit yourself to it.
10

10

00:00:17,970  -->  00:00:21,000
So again, as I said, if you do a quick Google search
11

11

00:00:21,000  -->  00:00:23,910
you'll find a lot of other bug bounty platforms.
12

12

00:00:23,910  -->  00:00:26,100
And there is no harm into signing up
13

13

00:00:26,100  -->  00:00:28,170
to as many as these platforms.
14

14

Because once you sign up to them,
15

15

16

17

18

Anyway, signing up is free

21

00:00:40,980  -->  00:00:42,960

22

22

00:00:42,960  -->  00:00:43,980
So in my opinion,

23

29

31

32

32

to start with as well.

33

00:01:05,160  -->  00:01:06,750
And honestly, in my opinion,

34

not because we are the owners of Bug Bounty,

35

36

37

38

38

39

39

40

40

41

You're not gonna make a lot of money

42

43

But like I said in the previous lecture,

44

45

45

00:01:33,330  -->  00:01:35,010

46

47

and you're gonna get familiar

48

100

124

125

126

127

00:04:21,270  -->  00:04:23,430

128

151

so that you know what you discover

154

155

You can also see here, it's very important

156

157

and the mobile application are not in scope.

158

So again, if you go to this website, the ZSVPN,

159

160

167

So anyway, the idea is the same.

1
1

00:00:00,000  -->  00:00:01,950
<v Instructor>So now that you know how to find</v>
2

2

00:00:01,950  -->  00:00:05,180
valid bug bounty programs that are legal to test
3

3

00:00:05,180  -->  00:00:08,010
and how to actually go ahead and test these programs,
4

4

00:00:08,010  -->  00:00:10,230
'cause that's what we covered in the whole course,
5

5

00:00:10,230  -->  00:00:13,500
let's assume that you actually managed to discover a bug
6

6

00:00:13,500  -->  00:00:16,950
and you wanna go ahead and submit a report for that bug.
7

7

00:00:16,950  -->  00:00:18,390
Now, before you go ahead
8

8

00:00:18,390  -->  00:00:20,340
and think about submitting the report
9

9

00:00:20,340  -->  00:00:22,800
there is a few things that you need to make sure
10

10

00:00:22,800  -->  00:00:24,660
that you have satisfied.
11

11

00:00:24,660  -->  00:00:25,830
First of all, you wanna make sure
12

12

00:00:25,830  -->  00:00:27,810
that the vulnerability is valid.
13

13

00:00:27,810  -->  00:00:31,290
Sometimes people discover bugs or not even vulnerabilities,
14

14

00:00:31,290  -->  00:00:33,390
they will be bugs that are actually part
15

15

00:00:33,390  -->  00:00:37,440
of the execution of the program or they're not actual bugs.
16

16

00:00:37,440  -->  00:00:39,870
So you wanna make sure that what you discovered is valid
17

17

00:00:39,870  -->  00:00:41,580
because there is nothing more embarrassing
18

18

00:00:41,580  -->  00:00:44,340
than send a report thinking that you discovered something
19

19

00:00:44,340  -->  00:00:46,950
and it's actually not a real valid bug,
20

20

00:00:46,950  -->  00:00:49,500
it's just a misunderstanding from your side.
21

21

00:00:49,500  -->  00:00:51,750
The next thing that you wanna make sure is
22

22

00:00:51,750  -->  00:00:54,820
that you wanna make sure that the vulnerability is in scope.
23

23

00:00:54,820  -->  00:00:57,315
And I mentioned that a few times in the previous lecture
24

24

00:00:57,315  -->  00:01:01,350
but this is very, very important because like I said,
25

25

00:01:01,350  -->  00:01:02,760
first of all it's illegal
26

26

00:01:02,760  -->  00:01:05,160
to testings that are outside of the scope.
27

27

00:01:05,160  -->  00:01:07,710
Second, you're gonna be wasting your time testing stuff
28

28

00:01:07,710  -->  00:01:09,030
outside of the scope.
29

29

00:01:09,030  -->  00:01:11,040
And third, you will never get a bounty for it.
30

30

00:01:11,040  -->  00:01:13,740
Sometimes you would, but in most cases you will not.
31

31

00:01:13,740  -->  00:01:15,960
You will just piss off the people that are managing
32

32

00:01:15,960  -->  00:01:17,460
the bug bounty program.
33

33

00:01:17,460  -->  00:01:20,400
So once you make sure that you discovered an actual bug
34

34

00:01:20,400  -->  00:01:23,820
or vulnerability that is within scope, you wanna go ahead
35

35

00:01:23,820  -->  00:01:26,220
to the program that you discovered the bug in.
36

36

00:01:26,220  -->  00:01:29,670
So we're assuming you discovered it in this program, ZSVPN,
37

37

00:01:29,670  -->  00:01:33,090
and you're gonna have to click on submit the report.
38

38

00:01:33,090  -->  00:01:35,040
Now obviously you're gonna have to be signed in
39

39

00:01:35,040  -->  00:01:36,570
to be able to do this,
40

40

00:01:36,570  -->  00:01:38,550
and once you do this you can see the process
41

41

00:01:38,550  -->  00:01:39,810
is simple enough.
42

42

00:01:39,810  -->  00:01:41,700
So you have your name, you have your email
43

43

00:01:41,700  -->  00:01:42,750
and you have a paragraph
44

44

00:01:42,750  -->  00:01:45,660
in here where you're supposed to detail your discovery.
45

45

00:01:45,660  -->  00:01:48,210
Now this could be slightly different dependent
46

46

00:01:48,210  -->  00:01:49,590
on the program as well.
47

47

00:01:49,590  -->  00:01:52,560
When you click on submit, you will actually see information
48

48

00:01:52,560  -->  00:01:55,137
in here telling you exactly what they want
49

49

00:01:55,137  -->  00:01:57,741
and you will be simply fill in in the blank.
50

50

00:01:57,741  -->  00:01:58,950
Other programs
51

51

00:01:58,950  -->  00:02:01,170
you might simply just have a blank right here
52

52

00:02:01,170  -->  00:02:03,030
and you have to fill it your way.
53

53

00:02:03,030  -->  00:02:05,310
But it's very important to remember
54

54

00:02:05,310  -->  00:02:07,830
that once you submit this, whatever report I have,
55

55

00:02:07,830  -->  00:02:10,380
once I submit it, it's gonna have to go through
56

56

00:02:10,380  -->  00:02:13,680
a review process where somebody is gonna have to read it.
57

57

00:02:13,680  -->  00:02:15,870
They will have to manually validate whatever
58

58

00:02:15,870  -->  00:02:17,520
that you wrote in here.
59

59

00:02:17,520  -->  00:02:19,920
If they cannot reproduce whatever you did
60

60

00:02:19,920  -->  00:02:22,500
they will get back to you, ask you questions,
61

61

00:02:22,500  -->  00:02:23,970
and you'll have to provide answers.
62

62

00:02:23,970  -->  00:02:27,390
And then they go ahead and perform more tests trying
63

63

00:02:27,390  -->  00:02:28,900
to replicate what you're saying.
64

64

00:02:28,900  -->  00:02:32,640
And all of that results into you waiting for longer for
65

65

00:02:32,640  -->  00:02:36,131
your bugs to be validated and for you to get your bounty.
66

66

00:02:36,131  -->  00:02:39,990
That's why it is very important to actually provide as much
67

67

00:02:39,990  -->  00:02:42,570
details as possible so that whenever they read
68

68

00:02:42,570  -->  00:02:45,270
the report for the first time, they will fully understand
69

69

00:02:45,270  -->  00:02:48,330
what you mean and they will be able to reproduce your bug
70

70

00:02:48,330  -->  00:02:51,330
without having to ask you any questions and therefore,
71

71

00:02:51,330  -->  00:02:53,580
you will get your bounty much quicker.
72

72

00:02:53,580  -->  00:02:55,890
So you wanna provide as much details as possible,
73

73

00:02:55,890  -->  00:02:59,040
you wanna provide screenshots, and I actually prefer
74

74

00:02:59,040  -->  00:03:00,960
and love to provide videos.
75

75

00:03:00,960  -->  00:03:04,470
And I know from our own team that they love to see videos
76

76

00:03:04,470  -->  00:03:07,500
because once we see a video we can see everything we want.
77

77

00:03:07,500  -->  00:03:08,940
So it's even better than screenshots
78

78

00:03:08,940  -->  00:03:10,650
because we can see all of the requests,
79

79

00:03:10,650  -->  00:03:12,930
we can see the full process that you're going through
80

80

00:03:12,930  -->  00:03:15,270
and we can easily see whether the bug
81

81

00:03:15,270  -->  00:03:17,880
that you are submitting is valid or not
82

82

00:03:17,880  -->  00:03:19,660
and whether it is in scope or not.
83

83

00:03:19,660  -->  00:03:21,990
And that's why we will be able to respond
84

84

00:03:21,990  -->  00:03:24,630
to you very quickly and hopefully you'll be able to
85

85

00:03:24,630  -->  00:03:26,640
get your bounty much quicker.
86

86

00:03:26,640  -->  00:03:28,530
So even like I said,
87

87

00:03:28,530  -->  00:03:31,260
there are some programs that will give you notes in here
88

88

00:03:31,260  -->  00:03:32,910
and just telling you to fill the blank
89

89

00:03:32,910  -->  00:03:34,150
into what they're asking for.
90

90

00:03:34,150  -->  00:03:37,890
I would still at the end of all of that provide more details
91

91

00:03:37,890  -->  00:03:40,200
if I felt like there is more details needed.
92

92

00:03:40,200  -->  00:03:43,830
And I would always, always provide a video because that,
93

93

00:03:43,830  -->  00:03:45,510
like I said, increases the chances
94

94

00:03:45,510  -->  00:03:47,460
of them fully understanding what I'm saying
95

95

00:03:47,460  -->  00:03:51,149
and giving me my bounty as quickly as possible.
96

96

00:03:51,149  -->  00:03:53,040
We're just gonna write whatever in here,
97

97

00:03:53,040  -->  00:03:54,390
we're just gonna say test, test.
98

98

00:03:54,390  -->  00:03:56,511
I just wanna show you what this is gonna look like.
99

99

00:03:56,511  -->  00:03:59,610
We're gonna say that we're not a robot, we're gonna agree
100

100

00:03:59,610  -->  00:04:03,270
to the terms, and submit the bounty or the report.
101

101

00:04:03,270  -->  00:04:05,940
And as you can see, you're gonna get more information
102

102

00:04:05,940  -->  00:04:08,460
on how this process is gonna go.
103

103

00:04:08,460  -->  00:04:10,830
So your report is gonna be manually reviewed
104

104

00:04:10,830  -->  00:04:13,020
and that's why we said you wanna provide
105

105

00:04:13,020  -->  00:04:14,479
as much information as possible.
106

106

00:04:14,479  -->  00:04:17,370
If it's valid, it's gonna change to awaiting payment.
107

107

00:04:17,370  -->  00:04:18,930
So that means that the team agreed
108

108

00:04:18,930  -->  00:04:21,480
that you discovered a valid bug and right now you're
109

109

00:04:21,480  -->  00:04:23,700
in the process of receiving your payment.
110

110

00:04:23,700  -->  00:04:25,770
Once the payment is received from the company
111

111

00:04:25,770  -->  00:04:27,960
it'll transfer to you and the status
112

112

00:04:27,960  -->  00:04:30,870
of the submission is gonna change to paid,
113

113

00:04:30,870  -->  00:04:34,020
and then the company is gonna be able to see your report.
114

114

00:04:34,020  -->  00:04:36,090
So during all of these steps
115

115

00:04:36,090  -->  00:04:38,910
the company is not gonna be able to see your report.
116

116

00:04:38,910  -->  00:04:41,580
We will be the only ones that will see your report
117

117

00:04:41,580  -->  00:04:44,250
and only once the company pays the bounty
118

118

00:04:44,250  -->  00:04:45,930
if it was a valid report,
119

119

00:04:45,930  -->  00:04:47,823
they will be able to see the report.
120

120

00:04:49,320  -->  00:04:53,320
Now you can actually go back and go to my reports in here
121

121

00:04:54,720  -->  00:04:55,770
and you'll be able to
122

122

00:04:55,770  -->  00:04:57,990
see the submission that we just submitted.
123

123

00:04:57,990  -->  00:04:59,190
We can see the date,
124

124

00:04:59,190  -->  00:05:02,700
we can see that the status right now is spending approval.
125

125

00:05:02,700  -->  00:05:05,640
And if you click on view you'll see the content.
126

126

00:05:05,640  -->  00:05:07,140
So obviously there'll be much more
127

127

00:05:07,140  -->  00:05:08,987
if you submitted a real valid bug.
128

128

00:05:08,987  -->  00:05:11,670
And if the team did not understand the bug
129

129

00:05:11,670  -->  00:05:14,880
or the report that you sent, they will be writing a reply
130

130

00:05:14,880  -->  00:05:17,100
for you here where you could also reply
131

131

00:05:17,100  -->  00:05:18,506
to them and add more attachments.
132

132

00:05:18,506  -->  00:05:19,830
And that's why I said
133

133

00:05:19,830  -->  00:05:21,720
if you don't provide enough information,
134

134

00:05:21,720  -->  00:05:23,430
this could go for very long
135

135

00:05:23,430  -->  00:05:25,779
and then you'll be waiting for your bounty for longer.
136

136

00:05:25,779  -->  00:05:29,130
That's why make sure that you provide as much information
137

137

00:05:29,130  -->  00:05:30,150
as possible.
138

138

00:05:30,150  -->  00:05:33,270
And that's it, this is really as simple as it gets.
139

139

00:05:33,270  -->  00:05:36,600
Let me quickly show you an example of submitting a report
140

140

00:05:36,600  -->  00:05:37,830
in HackerOne.
141

141

00:05:37,830  -->  00:05:39,900
So this is an example of Linktree.
142

142

00:05:39,900  -->  00:05:41,430
And we're gonna click on submit report.
143

143

00:05:41,430  -->  00:05:44,280
And like I said, the submit report changes
144

144

00:05:44,280  -->  00:05:45,600
dependent on the company.
145

145

00:05:45,600  -->  00:05:47,760
So this one might look a bit more complex than
146

146

00:05:47,760  -->  00:05:50,220
other companies because this is the way that they
147

147

00:05:50,220  -->  00:05:51,390
want it to be.
148

148

00:05:51,390  -->  00:05:53,970
So in here they're asking you for the asset,
149

149

00:05:53,970  -->  00:05:56,430
the domain that you discover the bug in.
150

150

00:05:56,430  -->  00:05:58,020
So let's just just click the first one
151

151

00:05:58,020  -->  00:06:00,300
assuming we discovered above in here.
152

152

00:06:00,300  -->  00:06:02,910
And then the next thing that is gonna ask you what type
153

153

00:06:02,910  -->  00:06:04,200
of a weakness it is.
154

154

00:06:04,200  -->  00:06:06,840
So let's say it is an access vulnerability.
155

155

00:06:06,840  -->  00:06:09,720
And then it's asking you to give it a rating.
156

156

00:06:09,720  -->  00:06:12,060
It's optional 'cause they will be modifying this
157

157

00:06:12,060  -->  00:06:13,740
once they get the response.
158

158

00:06:13,740  -->  00:06:16,440
So you can leave this or you can set it if you want.
159

159

00:06:16,440  -->  00:06:17,760
And then you're gonna get
160

160

00:06:17,760  -->  00:06:19,620
to the part that we were talking about.
161

161

00:06:19,620  -->  00:06:20,850
So you wanna give it a title.
162

162

00:06:20,850  -->  00:06:22,710
We're gonna say XSS, for example,
163

163

00:06:22,710  -->  00:06:25,260
you discovered a reflected XSS or whatever.
164

164

00:06:25,260  -->  00:06:28,680
Again, always provide as much information as possible,
165

165

00:06:28,680  -->  00:06:30,389
give meaningful names, even the title,
166

166

00:06:30,389  -->  00:06:32,310
make sure it is meaningful.
167

167

00:06:32,310  -->  00:06:34,830
All of this makes it easier to review your report
168

168

00:06:34,830  -->  00:06:37,575
which will result into you getting your bounty quicker.
169

169

00:06:37,575  -->  00:06:39,600
And then in here you can see
170

170

00:06:39,600  -->  00:06:41,790
that they are actually telling you what they want.
171

171

00:06:41,790  -->  00:06:43,050
So they're kind of making it seem
172

172

00:06:43,050  -->  00:06:45,300
like it's a fill the blank kind of thing.
173

173

00:06:45,300  -->  00:06:47,443
So you have to give them the steps in here,
174

174

00:06:47,443  -->  00:06:50,130
they're asking for the attachments in here,
175

175

00:06:50,130  -->  00:06:52,680
and then you wanna write the impact in here as well.
176

176

00:06:52,680  -->  00:06:55,290
What were you able to do with that vulnerability?
177

177

00:06:55,290  -->  00:06:57,870
Now there is programs where this could be much longer
178

178

00:06:57,870  -->  00:06:59,790
where you will have to write so much more
179

179

00:06:59,790  -->  00:07:01,170
than what they're asking in here.
180

180

00:07:01,170  -->  00:07:03,150
So this is kind of just the basic stuff that you
181

181

00:07:03,150  -->  00:07:04,500
would get asked for anyway.
182

182

00:07:04,500  -->  00:07:05,970
You would've provided this anyway.
183

183

00:07:05,970  -->  00:07:07,530
But like I said, even
184

184

00:07:07,530  -->  00:07:10,080
if they are not asking for a lot of information
185

185

00:07:10,080  -->  00:07:12,870
make sure you put a video and make sure you put screenshots
186

186

00:07:12,870  -->  00:07:15,930
and provide as much information as possible.
187

187

00:07:15,930  -->  00:07:18,270
If you scroll down again, there's some optional stuff,
188

188

00:07:18,270  -->  00:07:19,860
how long it took you to discover it,
189

189

00:07:19,860  -->  00:07:21,260
it's not really that important.
190

190

00:07:21,260  -->  00:07:23,520
And then in many other cases, for example,
191

191

00:07:23,520  -->  00:07:25,120
if you look at the PayPal program
192

192

00:07:25,120  -->  00:07:28,290
there is much more information that you will be asked for
193

193

00:07:28,290  -->  00:07:29,700
but nothing out of the ordinary.
194

194

00:07:29,700  -->  00:07:32,130
If you manage to discover a bug in that platform
195

195

00:07:32,130  -->  00:07:33,660
then you are familiar enough with it
196

196

00:07:33,660  -->  00:07:36,870
and you will be able to fill up that information based on
197

197

00:07:36,870  -->  00:07:39,030
the bug that you discovered.
198

198

00:07:39,030  -->  00:07:41,790
So again, do not get intimidated by HackerOne.
199

199

00:07:41,790  -->  00:07:43,530
It's a much bigger platform.
200

200

00:07:43,530  -->  00:07:46,140
I would highly recommend starting with smaller platforms
201

201

00:07:46,140  -->  00:07:48,180
like Bug Bounty and other smaller ones
202

202

00:07:48,180  -->  00:07:50,730
and then build up your skills, build up your confidence
203

203

00:07:50,730  -->  00:07:53,550
and move on to bigger bug bounty platforms
204

204

00:07:53,550  -->  00:07:55,040
or bigger bug bounty programs.
205

205

00:07:55,040  -->  00:07:58,200
But at the same time, there is no harm into signing up
206

206

00:07:58,200  -->  00:08:00,630
to all of these platforms because like I said,
207

207

00:08:00,630  -->  00:08:02,610
the more platforms you sign up for,
208

208

00:08:02,610  -->  00:08:04,410
the more programs that you have,
209

209

00:08:04,410  -->  00:08:06,450
therefore you have more platforms to test
210

210

00:08:06,450  -->  00:08:07,830
and therefore your chances
211

211

00:08:07,830  -->  00:08:10,863
of discovering bugs and getting bounties are much higher.
